items.title: 'Like I'd been ripped off': Life after a Parkinson's diagnosis at 41

children.content: Experts are predicting Parkinson's disease to double in the next 20 years, but little is known about what causes the condition and how to cure it. Tasmania has been chosen to contribute blood samples to a global study aimed at improving understanding of the genetics behind Parkinson's disease.

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a pony tail and grey jumper is backlit in her front yard with orange and yellow leaves illuminated behind her.

items.title: Will Ruth Forrest get an apology for her leaked email?

children.content: The Tasmanian upper house member was livid on Tuesday night after an email she sent to the government was leaked to the media. She wants an apology, but will she get one?

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair looks across the room

items.title: Cancer survivor's disappointment over hospital funding promise

children.content: A cancer survivor who stood alongside the Tasmanian premier at a 2025 election promise announcement says he is disappointed that the hospitals investment appears to be "on the back burner."

image.altText: Four people stand together outside.

items.title: 'People do care': Why private owners are handing land back

children.content: It has been two decades since substantial land hand-backs in Tasmania, so private owners are doing it instead. Near Hobart, a piece of land that was once a dumping ground is being restored.

items.title: Celine Cremer searchers turn attention to missing man Peter Willoughby

children.content: A group of volunteers, which played a major role in finding the remains of a lost Belgian tourist earlier this year, will today begin searching for another missing person in Tasmania's wilderness.

image.altText: A search party walks towards the camera

items.title: Farmers desperate for nitrogen 'drug' search for substitutes

children.content: A war thousands of kilometres away from Australia's farms is quietly reshaping what crops are planted and what will be available at supermarkets in the future.

image.altText: Man stands for portrait among apple orchard

items.title: TassieCast 🔊 Housing failures, why are we still falling short?

children.content: One in three Tassie households are struggling with rental affordability ... if you can find somewhere to rent, that is.

items.title: 'Heart ripped out on repeat': Auskick parents say they have been failed by AFL

children.content: The ABC has spoken to several families who say they have been failed by the AFL Integrity Unit following bullying, racism and harassment.

items.title: Mount Isa mum calls for more support after birth of premature twins

children.content: Mount Isa mother Kristen Curd spent four months in Townsville after giving birth to premature twin boys, and says the hardest part began when they finally came home.

image.altText: A mother holding her twin boys, standing infront of a Royal Flying Doctor Service aeroplane.

items.title: Leaked data shows NT diphtheria cases surged a month before alert was issued

children.content: Internal Northern Territory Health data leaked to the ABC reveals diphtheria case numbers surged in the NT a month before a public health alert was issued. The data also offers the first public insight into the regions hit hardest by the outbreak.

image.altText: A nurse injecting a needle into the arm of a patient who is getting vaccinated.

items.title: 'Stop the madness': Brisbane locals take Olympic stadium fight to IOC

children.content: Opponents of Brisbane's centrepiece Olympic stadium take their concerns directly to the IOC's headquarters in Switzerland as a critical deadline looms.

gallery.altText: A group of protesters outside the IOC headquarters in Switzerland holding up signs.

thumbnail.altText: A group of protesters outside the IOC headquarters in Switzerland holding up signs.

items.title: Big-wave surfing 'pioneer', 43, dies at remote NSW break, doing what he loved

children.content: Tributes mount for Nathan Bartlett, who chased epic waves around the world but died at a rugged spot near Jervis Bay, not far from where he grew up, on Wednesday.

image.altText: Nathan Bartlett is smiling and wearing a wide brim hat and sunglasses.

items.title: First of five survivors rescued from flooded Laos cave

children.content: Rescue divers in Laos have safely evacuated the first of five local villagers who had been trapped in a cave for more than a week by floodwaters.

image.altText: A video grab showing a rescuer evacuating the first of five villagers trapped in a cave.

items.title: The simple, ordinary joy of Kylie Minogue

children.content: In the end, a woman who's had wins and losses, terrors and triumphs, and who regularly goes back to stay with family in suburban Melbourne, has become a music legend of her time, and it's not hard to figure out why.

items.title: How women over 60 are still being made the butt of the joke in film

children.content: Films are four times more likely to feature a talking animal lead than a woman over 60. These charts explain the state of film for older women.

items.title: Heat during abnormally warm May for eastern states could be here to stay

children.content: Australia's east is coming to the end of an abnormally warm May, and the heat could be here to stay.

items.title: Canadian man accused of selling chemical online admits aiding suicide

children.content: In 2023, Interpol alerted Australian police that packages sold by the man, which contained a lethal substance, were sent to Australia, prompting authorities to check on the welfare of recipients.

items.title: Former mayor's lawyer describes private prosecution as 'nightmare'

items.title: Ski resorts remain optimistic despite forecast for warmer winter

children.content: Alpine business owners and resort managers say they are not overly concerned about a warmer winter forecast, with preparations underway for the 2026 ski season ahead of the King's Birthday long weekend.

items.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on launch pad during test

children.content: Blue Origin says all personnel are accounted for after experiencing an "anomaly", a term commonly used by rocket companies to describe a launch failure or explosion.

items.title: Helping 'big personality' marsupial recover after bushfires left them homeless

children.content: Artificial habitats are giving the Kangaroo Island dunnart a chance at recovery, after bushfires left them largely homeless.

items.title: How IVF could spell doom for feral rabbits

children.content: Scientists are using rabbit IVF and gene-editing technology to crash Australia's feral rabbit population by spreading infertility through female rabbits.

items.title: Hopes pinned on promising sequel to prevent a Whyalla wipeout

children.content: "We've seen this movie before" is a phrase that sums up the way many people feel about the Whyalla steelworks' financial predicament but the community is hopeful of a promising new sequel.

items.title: Are you allowed to make deepfakes of the prime minister?

children.content: Are you allowed to make deepfakes of the prime minister?
You might have seen AI generated memes of Albanese on social media following the announcement of the federal budget.
Whether or not those are legal comes down to what the post is actually communicating to the people seeing it online.

items.title: Eight students arrested over deadly girls' school fire in Kenya

items.title: Why younger generations are worse off than baby boomers

children.content: According to research from the Actuaries Institute, inequality between the younger and older generations is widening and could reach record levels within years.
